metadata.title: Research the Motivation Architecture with Claude: The Five Whys Applied to College
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: I'm in college for a degree is a strategy, not a motivation. The fifth why finds the answer loud enough to power 1 AM.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: I'm in college for a degree is a strategy, not a motivation. The fifth why finds the answer loud enough to power 1 AM.

beats.narration_text: The Five Whys exercise is not introspection — it is a decision procedure. The first answer is too abstract to compete with the immediate cost of staying up to finish problem set 7. The fifth answer is specific enough to do that work. The difference between students who push through hard semesters and students who don't is usually found in the fifth answer, not the first.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The Five Whys exercise is not introspection — it is a decision procedure. The first answer is too abstract to compete with the immediate cost of staying up to f

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude to run the Five Whys — five rounds of follow-up, then a synthesis paragraph.

props.command: claude "I want to do the Five Whys exercise to find my real motivation for being in college. Start by asking me 'Why are you in college?' then, based on my answer, ask a specific follow-up for each of 5 rounds. After 5 rounds, summarize what each round revealed — especially what the fifth answer implies about my underlying purpose. Then ask me to write a paragraph responding to your summary."

beats.narration_text: The 5-round dialogue transcript: Round 1 for a degree, Round 2 to get a job, Round 3 to be financially stable, Round 4 so my family doesn't have to worry, Round 5 because I'm the first person in my family to have this chance and I don't want to waste it.

props.code:
Round 1: "Why are you in college?"  → "For a degree."
Round 2: "Why do you want a degree?" → "To get a job."
Round 3: "Why that job?"             → "To be financially stable."
Round 4: "Why does stability matter?"→ "So my family doesn't worry."
Round 5: "Why does that matter most?"→ "I'm the first in my family
                                         with this chance. I can't
                                         waste it."

Synthesis: Your fifth answer is not a goal.
It is a debt you feel you owe.
At 1 AM, this answer will compete; the degree won't.

Student: "Yes. I've never said it out loud before."

beats.narration_text: Round 5 answer for the speech pathology example: I want to give kids the voice I wished I'd had access to when I was struggling. Claude's synthesis: Your fifth answer is a specific, irreversible relationship to children who are experiencing what you experienced. This is not a career goal — it is a debt you feel you owe. At 1 AM, this answer will compete with the cost; the degree won't. Student evaluation: Yes, that's exactly it — but I've never said it out loud before.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Round 5 answer for the speech pathology example: I want to give kids the voice I wished I'd had access to when I was struggling. Claude's synthesis: Your fifth

beats.narration_text: Run the Five Whys on uncertainty: I'm not sure why I'm in college.

props.command: claude "I'm not sure why I'm in college. Run the Five Whys on my uncertainty — not to push past it, but to map its structure. What are the distinct parts of my indecision, and which one feels most urgent?"

beats.narration_text: Uncertainty version: Claude identifies the structure of the indecision rather than pushing through it — your uncertainty has three parts: you're not sure if this is the right institution, you're not sure if you chose the right major, and you're not sure if the credential is worth the cost. These are three different problems. Which one feels most urgent? The Five Whys on uncertainty produces a map of the indecision, not an answer — which is the correct output when the answer doesn't exist yet.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Uncertainty version: Claude identifies the structure of the indecision rather than pushing through it — your uncertainty has three parts: you're not sure if thi

beats.narration_text: The first answer is the strategy. The fifth answer is the motivation. They are different things, and only the fifth answer is loud enough to compete with the cost. The Five Whys takes 15 minutes and produces the transcript that is worth returning to in semester two, when the cost is higher and the reason feels farther away.

beats.narration_text: Next: research why learning styles feel true — and what the evidence actually says.
